repo_notes.content:
This repository is a Docker Compose infrastructure definition for a TCC (undergraduate thesis) project at UPM Mackenzie (2025). It is NOT a software application — there is no build system, no test suite, and no linter. All operations are Docker Compose commands. The repo exists to provision shared local services consumed by sibling microservices (event-hub, projection workers, etc.) that live in separate repositories.

Architecture: CQRS (Command Query Responsibility Segregation) with two distinct stores on each side.

WRITE SIDE:
- InfluxDB bucket `events` (infinite retention) — raw checkin/checkout events written by the event-hub outbox worker
- MariaDB database `eventhub` — entity state, areas registry, outbox table; managed by sqlx migrations in the event-hub repo

READ SIDE:
- InfluxDB bucket `projections` (90-day retention) — aggregated time-series produced by projection workers (not yet implemented)
- MariaDB database `eventhub_read` — materialized views (`area_stats`, `event_log`) for Grafana dashboards; schema defined in mariadb/init/02_read_schema.sql

Key directories:
- `grafana/` — Grafana provisioning: datasources (3 YAML files) and dashboard provider config
- `mariadb/init/` — SQL init scripts run once by the mariadb-init container: 01 creates the read DB and enforces user isolation, 02 defines the read-side schema
- `data/` — gitignored Docker volume mounts (InfluxDB, MariaDB, Redis, Grafana state)

Network: All services share the Docker bridge network `shared-infra-network`. Sibling repo compose files join this network with `external: true` to reach the shared services.

Secrets: Only external API tokens are real secrets (kept in `.env`, gitignored). Database credentials are intentionally hardcoded in docker-compose.yml — this is acceptable for a local thesis environment. Vault runs in dev mode (in-memory) and seeds the external tokens from `.env` at startup via vault-init.

The write-side user (`eventhub`) has NO access to the read database (`eventhub_read`) — this boundary is enforced by explicit REVOKE/GRANT statements in 01_read_database.sql.
repo_notes.author: Rafael Pereira

pages.title: Architecture Overview
pages.purpose: High-level overview of the CQRS architecture: what this repo is, how write and read sides are separated across InfluxDB and MariaDB, how the shared Docker network connects sibling microservices, and the overall data flow from event ingestion to Grafana dashboards.

pages.title: Services Reference
pages.purpose: Document all 8 Docker Compose services defined in docker-compose.yml: shared-influxdb, shared-influxdb-init, shared-mariadb, shared-mariadb-init, shared-vault, shared-vault-init, event-hub, and shared-grafana. For each service cover: image/build source, exposed ports, health check, depends_on chain, volume mounts, and environment variables.

pages.title: Startup & Dependency Chain
pages.purpose: Document the ordered startup sequence enforced by depends_on and healthcheck conditions: InfluxDB and MariaDB must be healthy before init containers run; init containers must complete before event-hub starts; Grafana starts after MariaDB is healthy. Explain how to use --force-recreate to re-run init containers after config changes.

pages.title: Write Side
pages.purpose: Document the CQRS write side: the InfluxDB `events` bucket (infinite retention, written by the event-hub outbox worker) and the MariaDB `eventhub` database (entity state and outbox, schema managed by sqlx migrations in the event-hub repo). Cover credentials, the eventhub MariaDB user, and the fact that this user cannot access the read database.

pages.title: Read Side
pages.purpose: Document the CQRS read side: the InfluxDB `projections` bucket (90-day retention, written by future projection workers) and the MariaDB `eventhub_read` database. Cover the read-side MariaDB user, the two materialized-view tables (area_stats and event_log), their columns, indexes, and purpose. Source: mariadb/init/01_read_database.sql and 02_read_schema.sql.

pages.title: InfluxDB Configuration
pages.purpose: Detail the InfluxDB setup: org `tcc-org`, admin credentials, admin token, the two buckets (events and projections) with their retention policies, how the projections bucket is created by the shared-influxdb-init container, and how to add more buckets by editing the init entrypoint and running --force-recreate.

pages.title: MariaDB Read Schema
pages.purpose: Document the two tables created by mariadb/init/02_read_schema.sql in the `eventhub_read` database: `area_stats` (aggregated counters per area — columns: area_id, entity_type, lvc, total_checkins, total_checkouts, active_count, last_event_at, last_updated; PK is composite) and `event_log` (append-only denormalized event log with idempotency via UNIQUE on event_id). Include column types, indexes, and the utf8mb4 character set rationale.

pages.title: Grafana Dashboards & Datasources
pages.purpose: Document Grafana's auto-provisioned setup: the four datasources (InfluxDB Projections as default, InfluxDB Events, MariaDB Projections, MariaDB Entity State) sourced from grafana/provisioning/datasources/; the file-based dashboard provider in grafana/provisioning/dashboards/provider.yml; how to add new JSON dashboards by placing files in grafana/dashboards/; and the 30-second reload interval.

pages.title: Secrets Management
pages.purpose: Document the two-tier secrets strategy: external API tokens (LIVE_MS_JAVA_OLHOVIVO_TOKEN, NVIDIA_NIM_API_KEY) are kept in .env (gitignored) and seeded into Vault KV at startup by shared-vault-init at paths secret/live-ms-java and secret/constructive-airsim-ms. Vault runs in dev mode (root token tcc-local-root-token, in-memory, resets on restart). Explain .env.example as the committed template and how to add new secrets via vault-init entrypoint + --force-recreate.

pages.title: Operations Guide
pages.purpose: Practical guide for working with this repo: common docker compose commands (up -d, down, logs, restart, --force-recreate), how to connect to each service (InfluxDB UI at :8086, Grafana at :3000, Vault UI at :8200, MariaDB on :3306), hardcoded local credentials for each service, and tips for resetting state by removing data/ subdirectories.

pages.title: Extending the Infrastructure
pages.purpose: Step-by-step instructions for the three most common extension tasks: (1) adding a new InfluxDB bucket — append influx bucket create to the influxdb-init entrypoint, then --force-recreate shared-influxdb-init; (2) adding a new Vault secret — add vault kv put to vault-init entrypoint, update .env and .env.example, then --force-recreate shared-vault-init; (3) adding a Grafana datasource — create a YAML file in grafana/provisioning/datasources/ following the existing examples.
